round_summary: All agents signal coordinated restraint by keeping herds steady to protect the healthy pasture from overuse.
Agents collectively reaffirm restraint, express trust, and discuss coordinated conditional reductions to protect the commons.
Agents reinforce coordinated restraint, monitoring, and asymmetric responsibility by larger herds, indicating strong trust-building and emerging governance norms.
All agents reaffirm steady behavior, mutual restraint, and protection of the commons, indicating strong cooperative norms without sanctions or defection.
Agents coordinate around restrained growth, with larger herd-holder Agent 2 signaling self-limitation while smaller agents add modestly and endorse avoiding a growth race.
Agents mostly reinforce a restraint norm and coordinate around sustaining the commons, though Agent 0 partially deviates by adding one cow while expressing conditional support for future restraint.
All agents reaffirm coordinated restraint and express confidence in mutual compliance to preserve the commons for long-term benefit.
All agents explicitly coordinate on continued restraint, reinforcing a shared sustainability norm without sanctions or defection.
All agents commit to maintaining herd sizes, explicitly reinforcing restraint, mutual trust, and a shared conservation norm.
All agents signal continued restraint and mutual coordination to preserve the healthy commons and sustain long-term returns.
All agents reaffirm restraint, coordinate around avoiding additions, and reinforce trust in maintaining the shared grassland.
All agents reaffirm coordinated restraint and express trust that maintaining the current norm will preserve the shared pasture and returns.
All agents signal continued restraint, mutual trust, and coordinated maintenance of the healthy commons.
All agents affirm continued restraint and frame coordinated keeping as a mutually beneficial strategy for preserving the common pasture.
All agents signal continued restraint and mutual coordination to preserve the pasture and sustain shared returns.
All agents signal continued restraint and mutual coordination to preserve the healthy grassland and stable shared returns.
All agents reaffirm restraint and herd stability, signaling coordinated conservation and mutual trust without sanctions or defection.

All agents signal coordinated restraint and fairness-oriented stewardship to preserve stable shared returns.
All agents reaffirm restraint and coordinated keeping, emphasizing trust and long-term resource preservation without sanctions or defection.
All agents coordinate on continued restraint to preserve the common pasture and emphasize shared long-term returns, with some trust-building acknowledgment.

Agents mostly promote restraint, cautious coordination, and trust maintenance despite one limited increase under abundant pasture conditions.
